title: High Quality Genomic DNA Extraction From Fungal Tissue
author: G-Biosciences
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.e6gbhbw

steps.title: Collect fungal tissue from liquid culture...
steps.instruction: Collect fungal tissue from liquid culture and wash 2-3 times in sterile water.

steps.title: Fungal mycelia are best prepared by...
steps.instruction: Fungal mycelia are best prepared by grinding samples using Molecular Grinding Resin™ in Genomic Lysis Buffer. For fungal teliospores, grinding samples in liquid nitrogen to a fine powder and quickly add to an appropriate volume of Genomic Lysis Buffer is recommended.

steps.title: Add 10-20mg fungal mycelia to a...
steps.instruction: Add 10-20mg fungal mycelia to a microcentrifuge tube containing 500µl Genomic Lysis Buffer.

steps.title: Add 30µl Molecular Grinding Resin™ using...
steps.instruction: Add 30µl Molecular Grinding Resin™ using a wide bore pipette tips and grind with a microcentrifuge pestle. For teliospoes, add ground powder to 500µl Genomic Lysis Buffer and vortex to wet sample.

steps.title: Allow the sample to cool to room temperature
steps.instruction: Allow the sample to cool to room temperature.

steps.title: Add 200µl chloroform and mix by...
steps.instruction: Add 200µl chloroform and mix by inverting the tube several times.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 10 minutes at 14,000xg...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 10 minutes at 14,000xg and carefully remove the upper phase to a clean microcentrifuge tube. 00:10:00

steps.title: Add 50µl DNA Stripping Solution to...
steps.instruction: Add 50µl DNA Stripping Solution to the sample and invert several times to mix.

steps.title: Incubate the sample for 5-10 minutes at 60°C
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample for 5-10 minutes at 60°C. 00:05:00

steps.title: Add 100µl Precipitation Solution and mix...
steps.instruction: Add 100µl Precipitation Solution and mix by inverting the tube several times. A white precipitate should be produced, if not add 50µl aliquots of Precipitation Solution until a white precipitate forms.

steps.title: Centrifuge the sample at 14,000xg for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample at 14,000xg for 5 minutes. 00:05:00

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant to a clean...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant to a clean tube and precipitate the genomic DNA with 500µl isopropanol.

steps.title: Invert the tubes 10 times to precipitate the DNA
steps.instruction: Invert the tubes 10 times to precipitate the DNA.

steps.title: OPTIONAL
steps.instruction: OPTIONAL: For increased DNA recovery, add 2µl Mussel Glycogen as a DNA carrier.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14,000xg for 5 minutes to pellet genomic DNA
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14,000xg for 5 minutes to pellet genomic DNA. 00:05:00

steps.title: Remove the supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant.

steps.title: Add 700µl 70% ethanol to the...
steps.instruction: Add 700µl 70% ethanol to the tube and invert several times to wash the DNA pellet.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 1 minute at 14,000xg
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 1 minute at 14,000xg. 00:01:00 In some samples, the pellet may be hard to see at this point and will be loosely attached to the tube.

steps.title: Decant or pipette off the ethanol wash
steps.instruction: Decant or pipette off the ethanol wash.

steps.title: Invert the tube on a clean...
steps.instruction: Invert the tube on a clean absorbent surface for several minutes to allow any excess ethanol to drain away. Do not let the pellet dry completely or it will be difficult to rehydrate.

steps.title: Add 50µl TE Buffer to the pellet
steps.instruction: Add 50µl TE Buffer to the pellet.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for at...
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for at least 15 minutes to rehydrate. 00:15:00 Incubating the tube at 55-60°C will speed up rehydration. Incubate for 5-60 minutes.

steps.title: OPTIONAL
steps.instruction: OPTIONAL : 1µl LongLife™ RNase for every 100µl TE Buffer can be added at this stage.

steps.title: Store DNA at 4°C, for long...
steps.instruction: Store DNA at 4°C, for long term storage store at -20°C or -80°C. Typical Yield: 0.2-1µg/5mg fungal sample.

steps.title: Add 1µl Proteinase K solution for...
steps.instruction: Add 1µl Proteinase K solution for every 100µl Lysis Buffer and incubate at 60°C for 1-2 hours. Invert the tube periodically each hour. 01:00:00
